creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: The talk presents a four-layer architecture for sustaining brand voice in AI products: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. This approach is contrasted with single-pass tone instructions, using examples from wedding venue, companion AI, and missing persons tools.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts 10,000+ notes from Obsidian vaults into live context for AI agents through three-layer memory architecture: raw content, indexed layer, and wiki layer. The system is designed to enable personalized deep research across personal knowledge bases and the open web while remaining token-efficient.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents a talk on building and scaling AI agents in production for government workflows. The session covers agent architecture, feedback and evaluation, long context handling, monitoring, tool integration, and development practices using Effect-TS and Typescript with Claude and Cursor.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack context persistence and codebase visibility, limiting their utility despite technical capability. He frames this as a critical gap that needs solving and introduces Nx and Polygraph as solutions for enabling agent autonomy in monorepos.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin breaks down Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model is only 10% of results while the harness of context, tools, and guardrails comprises 90%. The video presents agentic engineering as superior to vibe coding and positions context engineering and orchestration skills as critical for 2026 AI coding workflows.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that exists outside individual AI tools, enabling persistent context and memories across different AI agents and providers. The service encrypts memories, ties them to user-owned keys, and allows selective access control between agents.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews the Kimi K2.7 Code model, an open-source coding-focused agentic model from Moonshot AI. He highlights its strong performance relative to its size and cost.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers are moving away from manual prompting toward self-directed agentic loops that prompt themselves repeatedly. These loops use orchestrator agents to spawn parallel worker agents, compound cost and hallucination risks, but enable hands-off automation at scale.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason announced a new project involving Crabbox, a codebase harness, and skills framework hosted on GitHub. The project is part of the AI Builder Club initiative and includes resources for step-by-step setup and learning at aibuilderclub.com.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains what Loop Engineer is and provides setup instructions. The video references a GitHub repository with a Loop Engineer template and a workshop on building AI agents, suggesting Loop Engineer is a development tool or framework for AI agent construction.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video explaining how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. Covers practical use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO, with tools like Loopany and memory systems enabling the approach.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews emerging autonomous coding tools including the /goal feature, Crewlet, and Superdesign. The video demonstrates how the /goal feature works for setting autonomous coding goals and discusses features like Goal-buddy and Mission exploration for handling extended multi-step tasks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: Educational video overview of generative pretrained transformer (GPT) mechanics. Covers self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, residual connections, and decoder architecture that powers models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: A video tutorial by Caleb Writes Code explaining Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model in ten minutes. The tutorial covers tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, optimization, and backpropagation as foundational concepts for understanding how language models process tokens, serving as a precursor to understanding GPT and attention mechanisms.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra model variants. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, Latent Mixture of Experts, and MTP (Multi-Token Prediction) techniques.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that introduces MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), replacing full attention with sparse attention. It includes tiled improvements and optimized KV cache management to reduce latency in both prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: This tutorial presents a system design interview methodology that reverses conventional approaches by prioritizing problem requirements over technology selection. The framework consists of seven sequential steps: understanding the problem, identifying requirements, predicting traffic patterns, designing high-level components, defining APIs, selecting a tech stack, and considering implementation constraints.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Demonstrates tool calling as the mechanism that transforms a chatbot into a functional AI agent. Explains why tools are necessary (stale training data, no file access, inability to take action), how the agent requests tools and frameworks execute them, and how system prompts guide tool selection. Covers tool anatomy including schema, description, and handler implementation.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a toolkit that equips AI coding agents with tools, documentation, best practices, and guardrails for interacting with AWS services. It provides up-to-date information and tested procedures to improve agent success rates and reduce inefficient effort.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS tutorial on running Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock, enabling on-premises deployment with data residency compliance. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, cost tracking, and content guardrails.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech comparing code generation practices at OpenAI and Claude versus Google, with projections on Google's approach to AI-assisted coding through 2026. The title frames adoption of AI coding as "vibing code," a colloquial term for casual or intuitive use of code generation tools.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech reviews Malus, a company offering an alternative business model around open source software. The video discusses how Malus's approach may reshape dynamics for open source maintainers and questions whether the model is fundamentally harmful to open source principles.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that software moats are built on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that AI's threat to SaaS companies is overstated and that the best AI products create passionate customers by solving specific problems like sales meeting intelligence.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that humanoid robotics may represent a narrow vision for the robotic future, and that designing robots for human delight and interaction rather than pure utility will be more important as billions of robots enter society. She questions the myopic focus of companies like 1X and Unitree on humanoid form factors.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against much larger incumbents by building a full enterprise platform from day one rather than starting with a single feature. She argues that even innovative companies like Google rely on outdated tools like spreadsheets for strategic planning, creating an opportunity for modern AI-powered performance management platforms.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit shows as customer pull rather than push, warns against mistaking passionate niche markets for true PMF, and advocates for embedded customer relationships over formal interviews. He also emphasizes the importance of betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof warns that businesses have overlooked critical security threats from agentic AI systems, particularly prompt injection attacks, unauthorized data deletion, and unintended actions. Despite AI's potential, organizational hesitation around deployment reflects inadequate risk assessment in the industry.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's "This Is Tech Year" podcast episode discusses Anthropic's latest developments in an episode recorded in Lisbon. The episode features guest Joao and covers current AI industry news.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: A commentary contrasting agile small teams with large teams, arguing that small units achieve higher productivity while large teams suffer from excessive meetings and coordination overhead.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The AI boom is driving strong demand for high bandwidth memory, with Micron and SK Hynix positioned to benefit from significant growth ahead. The video discusses how these memory manufacturers are shaping the future of the memory stack for AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
